sections.paragraph:
ὄνειρον, ου, τό and ὄνειρος, ου, ὁ (cp. ὄναρ; Hom.+; ins, pap, LXX; EpArist 192 and 315; En 13:8 [ὄνειροι]; TestLevi 8:18; TestAbr; SibOr 3, 293; Philo, Joseph.; Just., A I, 14, 1; Tat.) dream ἄγγελος κυρίου φαίνεται αὐτῷ κατʼ ὄνειρον an angel of the Lord appeared to (Joseph) in a dream GJs 14:2 (cp. κατʼ ὄναρ Mt 1:20). Cp. the def. Artem. 1, 2 p. 5 H. ὄνειρός ἐστι κίνησις ἢ πλάσις ψυχῆς πολυσχήμων σημαντικὴ τῶν ἐσομένων ἀγαθῶν ἢ κακῶν ‘a dream is a movement or formation of the soul signifying in a variety of ways those things that will be good or bad.’—DELG s.v. ὄναρ.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

def. def. = definition

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 711
